scenes.text:
People decide who you are in the
first seven seconds. Make them count.

Before you say a single word, your body has already spoken. Your posture, your eye contact, your handshake, the way you walk into a
room. Studies show that standing in a power pose for two minutes increases testosterone by twenty percent and decreases cortisol by twenty-five percent.

Stand tall. Shoulders back. Chin up. Walk like you own the room. Not arrogance.
Presence. There is a difference between confidence and cockiness. One inspires, the other repels.

Your body teaches your brain how to feel.
Stand like a champion, feel like a champion.

Try the power pose for two minutes
right now. Follow for confidence science.
